# Deep Research: OAuth Integration (Claude + Codex) via Mastra Custom Gateways

**Date:** 2026-03-08  
**Status:** Research Complete  
**Purpose:** Implement OAuth/Token-based authentication for Claude (Anthropic) and Codex (OpenAI) using Mastra Custom Gateways

---

## Executive Summary

Mastra's **Custom Gateway** system is the correct abstraction for integrating LLM providers with custom authentication (including OAuth). Extend `MastraModelGateway` to create a gateway that handles token exchange, caching, and model resolution for Claude and Codex.

This approach differs from server-side Mastra auth (ISSOProvider/MastraAuthProvider) — **gateways are for LLM provider authentication**, not user authentication.

---

## 1. Understanding Mastra Custom Gateways

### 1.1 Architecture

**MastraModelGateway** (abstract base class from `@mastra/core/llm`):

```typescript
abstract class MastraModelGateway {
  abstract readonly id: string;           // Gateway ID (used as prefix: "oauth-claude")
  abstract readonly name: string;          // Display name

  abstract fetchProviders(): Promise<Record<string, ProviderConfig>>;
  abstract buildUrl(modelId: string, envVars?: Record<string, string>): 
    string | undefined | Promise<string | undefined>;
  abstract getApiKey(modelId: string): Promise<string>;
  abstract resolveLanguageModel(args: {
    modelId: string;
    providerId: string;
    apiKey: string;
    headers?: Record<string, string>;
  }): Promise<GatewayLanguageModel>;
}
```

**ProviderConfig** interface:

```typescript
interface ProviderConfig {
  name: string;                    // "Claude" or "Codex"
  models: string[];                // ["claude-3-sonnet", ...]
  apiKeyEnvVar: string | string[]; // "CLAUDE_ACCESS_TOKEN" or "OPENAI_ACCESS_TOKEN"
  gateway: string;                 // Gateway ID
  url?: string;                    // API base URL
  apiKeyHeader?: string;           // Custom header (default: "Authorization")
  docUrl?: string;
}
```

### 1.2 Model ID Format

```
[gateway-id]/[provider]/[model]

Example:
oauth-claude/anthropic/claude-3-sonnet-20250219
oauth-codex/openai/gpt-5-codex
```

### 1.3 Real Examples from Mastra

**NetlifyGateway** (token caching + multi-provider):
- Caches tokens in memory with expiration
- Fetches site-specific tokens from Netlify API
- Uses `getOrFetchToken()` pattern
- Implements auto-refresh logic

**AzureOpenAIGateway** (OAuth with service principal):
- Uses Azure AD token exchange
- Caches management tokens
- Fetches deployments dynamically
- Implements credential validation

---

## 2. Claude OAuth via Custom Gateway

### 2.1 Anthropic Authentication Options

#### Option A: API Key (Simplest, MVP)
- Static key from console.anthropic.com
- No token refresh needed
- Use: `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...`

#### Option B: OAuth Token (Enterprise/Future)
- Generated via `anthropic auth login` or OAuth flow
- Supports token refresh
- Bearer token format: `Authorization: Bearer <token>`
- Endpoint: `https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages`

#### Option C: Device Code Flow (CLI/Headless)
- Alternative for environments without browser
- User enters code at https://cloud.anthropic.com/auth/device
- Returns access token + refresh token

### 2.2 Gateway Implementation (Claude)

```typescript
// File: claude-oauth-gateway.ts
import { MastraModelGateway } from '@mastra/core/llm'
import type { ProviderConfig, GatewayLanguageModel } from '@mastra/core/llm'
import { createAnthropic } from '@ai-sdk/anthropic'
import type { LanguageModelV2 } from '@ai-sdk/provider-v5'

interface TokenCache {
  accessToken: string
  refreshToken?: string
  expiresAt: number
}

export class ClaudeOAuthGateway extends MastraModelGateway {
  readonly id = 'oauth-claude'
  readonly name = 'Claude OAuth Gateway'
  
  private tokenCache: Map<string, TokenCache> = new Map()
  private clientId: string
  private clientSecret: string
  private redirectUri: string

  constructor(options?: {
    clientId?: string
    clientSecret?: string
    redirectUri?: string
  }) {
    super()
    this.clientId = options?.clientId ?? process.env.CLAUDE_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID ?? ''
    this.clientSecret = options?.clientSecret ?? process.env.CLAUDE_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET ?? ''
    this.redirectUri = options?.redirectUri ?? process.env.CLAUDE_OAUTH_REDIRECT_URI ?? ''
  }

  async fetchProviders(): Promise<Record<string, ProviderConfig>> {
    // Return available Claude models
    return {
      anthropic: {
        name: 'Anthropic Claude',
        models: [
          'claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022',
          'claude-3-5-haiku-20241022',
          'claude-3-opus-20250219',
        ],
        apiKeyEnvVar: 'CLAUDE_ACCESS_TOKEN',
        gateway: this.id,
        url: 'https://api.anthropic.com/v1',
        apiKeyHeader: 'x-api-key', // Anthropic uses x-api-key header
        docUrl: 'https://docs.anthropic.com',
      },
    }
  }

  buildUrl(
    modelId: string,
    envVars?: Record<string, string>
  ): string | undefined {
    // Return Anthropic API base URL
    if (modelId.includes('anthropic')) {
      return 'https://api.anthropic.com/v1'
    }
    return undefined
  }

  async getApiKey(modelId: string): Promise<string> {
    // Try environment variable first (simple case)
    const envKey = process.env.CLAUDE_ACCESS_TOKEN || process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
    if (envKey) {
      return envKey
    }

    // If OAuth configured, try cached/refreshed token
    if (this.clientId && this.clientSecret) {
      return await this.getOrRefreshToken(modelId)
    }

    throw new Error(
      'No Claude authentication found. ' +
      'Set CLAUDE_ACCESS_TOKEN/ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or configure OAuth (CLAUDE_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID, etc.)'
    )
  }

  private async getOrRefreshToken(modelId: string): Promise<string> {
    const cached = this.tokenCache.get(modelId)

    // Return cached token if still valid (5min buffer)
    if (cached && cached.expiresAt > Date.now() + 300000) {
      return cached.accessToken
    }

    // If refresh token available, refresh
    if (cached?.refreshToken) {
      const refreshed = await this.refreshAccessToken(cached.refreshToken)
      this.tokenCache.set(modelId, refreshed)
      return refreshed.accessToken
    }

    // No valid token — would need user OAuth flow here
    throw new Error('Token expired and no refresh token available')
  }

  private async refreshAccessToken(
    refreshToken: string
  ): Promise<TokenCache> {
    const response = await fetch('https://api.anthropic.com/oauth/token', {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      body: JSON.stringify({
        grant_type: 'refresh_token',
        refresh_token: refreshToken,
        client_id: this.clientId,
        client_secret: this.clientSecret,
      }),
    })

    if (!response.ok) {
      throw new Error('Token refresh failed')
    }

    const data = await response.json() as any
    return {
      accessToken: data.access_token,
      refreshToken: data.refresh_token,
      expiresAt: Date.now() + (data.expires_in ?? 3600) * 1000,
    }
  }

  async resolveLanguageModel({
    modelId,
    providerId,
    apiKey,
  }: {
    modelId: string
    providerId: string
    apiKey: string
  }): Promise<GatewayLanguageModel> {
    // Extract model name from full ID (e.g., "oauth-claude/anthropic/claude-3-sonnet" → "claude-3-sonnet")
    const model = modelId.split('/').pop() || modelId

    // Create Anthropic SDK instance with API key
    return createAnthropic({
      apiKey,
    }).chatModel(model) as any as GatewayLanguageModel
  }
}
```

---

## 3. Codex OAuth via Custom Gateway

### 3.1 OpenAI Authentication Options

#### Option A: API Key (Simplest, MVP)
- Direct key from platform.openai.com
- Use: `OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...`

#### Option B: OAuth with ChatGPT Sign-In
- Redirect to https://platform.openai.com/oauth/authorize
- Exchange code for access token
- Supports ChatGPT workspace permissions
- Tokens auto-refresh during active sessions

#### Option C: Device Code Auth (Headless)
- Alternative for CLI/server environments
- User receives device code to enter at openai.com

### 3.2 Gateway Implementation (Codex)

```typescript
// File: codex-oauth-gateway.ts
import { MastraModelGateway } from '@mastra/core/llm'
import type { ProviderConfig, GatewayLanguageModel } from '@mastra/core/llm'
import { createOpenAICompatible } from '@ai-sdk/openai-compatible-v5'
import type { LanguageModelV2 } from '@ai-sdk/provider-v5'

interface TokenCache {
  accessToken: string
  refreshToken?: string
  expiresAt: number
}

export class CodexOAuthGateway extends MastraModelGateway {
  readonly id = 'oauth-codex'
  readonly name = 'Codex OAuth Gateway'
  
  private tokenCache: Map<string, TokenCache> = new Map()
  private clientId: string
  private clientSecret: string
  private redirectUri: string

  constructor(options?: {
    clientId?: string
    clientSecret?: string
    redirectUri?: string
  }) {
    super()
    this.clientId = options?.clientId ?? process.env.OPENAI_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID ?? ''
    this.clientSecret = options?.clientSecret ?? process.env.OPENAI_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET ?? ''
    this.redirectUri = options?.redirectUri ?? process.env.OPENAI_OAUTH_REDIRECT_URI ?? ''
  }

  async fetchProviders(): Promise<Record<string, ProviderConfig>> {
    return {
      openai: {
        name: 'OpenAI Codex',
        models: [
          'gpt-5.3-codex',
          'gpt-5.2-codex',
          'gpt-5-codex',
          'gpt-4o',
          'gpt-4o-mini',
        ],
        apiKeyEnvVar: 'OPENAI_ACCESS_TOKEN',
        gateway: this.id,
        url: 'https://api.openai.com/v1',
        apiKeyHeader: 'Authorization', // "Bearer <token>"
        docUrl: 'https://platform.openai.com/docs',
      },
    }
  }

  buildUrl(
    modelId: string,
    envVars?: Record<string, string>
  ): string | undefined {
    if (modelId.includes('openai')) {
      return 'https://api.openai.com/v1'
    }
    return undefined
  }

  async getApiKey(modelId: string): Promise<string> {
    // Try environment variable first
    const envKey = process.env.OPENAI_ACCESS_TOKEN || process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY
    if (envKey) {
      return envKey
    }

    // If OAuth configured, try cached/refreshed token
    if (this.clientId && this.clientSecret) {
      return await this.getOrRefreshToken(modelId)
    }

    throw new Error(
      'No OpenAI authentication found. ' +
      'Set OPENAI_API_KEY/OPENAI_ACCESS_TOKEN or configure OAuth (OPENAI_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID, etc.)'
    )
  }

  private async getOrRefreshToken(modelId: string): Promise<string> {
    const cached = this.tokenCache.get(modelId)

    // Return cached token if still valid (5min buffer)
    if (cached && cached.expiresAt > Date.now() + 300000) {
      return cached.accessToken
    }

    // If refresh token available, refresh
    if (cached?.refreshToken) {
      const refreshed = await this.refreshAccessToken(cached.refreshToken)
      this.tokenCache.set(modelId, refreshed)
      return refreshed.accessToken
    }

    throw new Error('Token expired and no refresh token available')
  }

  private async refreshAccessToken(
    refreshToken: string
  ): Promise<TokenCache> {
    const response = await fetch('https://api.openai.com/v1/oauth/token', {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      body: JSON.stringify({
        grant_type: 'refresh_token',
        refresh_token: refreshToken,
        client_id: this.clientId,
        client_secret: this.clientSecret,
      }),
    })

    if (!response.ok) {
      throw new Error('Token refresh failed')
    }

    const data = await response.json() as any
    return {
      accessToken: data.access_token,
      refreshToken: data.refresh_token,
      expiresAt: Date.now() + (data.expires_in ?? 3600) * 1000,
    }
  }

  async resolveLanguageModel({
    modelId,
    providerId,
    apiKey,
  }: {
    modelId: string
    providerId: string
    apiKey: string
  }): Promise<GatewayLanguageModel> {
    const model = modelId.split('/').pop() || modelId

    // Create OpenAI-compatible SDK instance
    return createOpenAICompatible({
      name: 'openai',
      apiKey,
      baseURL: 'https://api.openai.com/v1',
      supportsStructuredOutputs: true,
    }).chatModel(model) as any as GatewayLanguageModel
  }
}
```

---

## 4. Integration into Mastra

### 4.1 Register Gateways

```typescript
// File: mastra.ts
import { Mastra } from '@mastra/core'
import { ClaudeOAuthGateway } from './gateways/claude-oauth-gateway'
import { CodexOAuthGateway } from './gateways/codex-oauth-gateway'

export const mastra = new Mastra({
  gateways: {
    claudeGateway: new ClaudeOAuthGateway({
      clientId: process.env.CLAUDE_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID,
      clientSecret: process.env.CLAUDE_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET,
      redirectUri: process.env.CLAUDE_OAUTH_REDIRECT_URI,
    }),
    codexGateway: new CodexOAuthGateway({
      clientId: process.env.OPENAI_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID,
      clientSecret: process.env.OPENAI_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET,
      redirectUri: process.env.OPENAI_OAUTH_REDIRECT_URI,
    }),
  },
})
```

### 4.2 Use in Agents

```typescript
import { Agent } from '@mastra/core/agent'

// Claude via OAuth gateway
const claudeAgent = new Agent({
  id: 'claude-agent',
  name: 'Claude Agent',
  instructions: 'You are a helpful assistant',
  model: 'oauth-claude/anthropic/claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022',
})

// Codex via OAuth gateway
const codexAgent = new Agent({
  id: 'codex-agent',
  name: 'Codex Agent',
  instructions: 'You are a code-writing assistant',
  model: 'oauth-codex/openai/gpt-5.3-codex',
})

mastra.addAgent(claudeAgent, 'claudeAgent')
mastra.addAgent(codexAgent, 'codexAgent')
```

---

## 5. OAuth Flow (If Implementing Full OAuth)

### 5.1 Server Endpoint for OAuth Callback

```typescript
// File: routes/auth/callback.ts
import { Mastra } from '@mastra/core'

export async function handleOAuthCallback(
  code: string,
  provider: 'claude' | 'codex',
  mastra: Mastra
) {
  const gateway = provider === 'claude'
    ? mastra.getGateway('claudeGateway')
    : mastra.getGateway('codexGateway')

  // Exchange code for token (implement in gateway's private method)
  const token = await (gateway as any).exchangeCodeForToken(code)

  // Store token securely (encrypted database, secure cookie, etc.)
  await saveTokenToDatabase(provider, token)

  return { success: true, token }
}
```

### 5.2 Token Storage Strategy

```typescript
// File: services/token-service.ts
import crypto from 'crypto'

interface StoredToken {
  provider: 'claude' | 'codex'
  accessToken: string
  refreshToken?: string
  expiresAt: number
  encrypted: boolean
}

async function saveTokenToDatabase(
  provider: 'claude' | 'codex',
  token: any
): Promise<void> {
  const encryptionKey = process.env.ENCRYPTION_KEY // 32-byte hex
  
  const encrypted = crypto
    .createCipheriv('aes-256-cbc', Buffer.from(encryptionKey, 'hex'), Buffer.alloc(16, 0))
    .update(JSON.stringify(token))
    .final()

  const stored: StoredToken = {
    provider,
    accessToken: encrypted.toString('hex'),
    refreshToken: token.refresh_token ? encrypted.toString('hex') : undefined,
    expiresAt: Date.now() + (token.expires_in ?? 3600) * 1000,
    encrypted: true,
  }

  // Save to database
  await db.tokens.insert(stored)
}
```

---

## 6. Environment Variables

```bash
# Claude OAuth (when implementing full OAuth)
CLAUDE_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID=...
CLAUDE_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET=...
CLAUDE_OAUTH_REDIRECT_URI=http://localhost:3000/auth/callback

# Claude Simple (MVP - API Key)
